cards.frontJa: ___ it was cold, we still sat on the terrace.
cards.backEn: Although it was cold, we still sat on the terrace.

cards.frontJa: ___ the ticket was expensive, the show was worth it.
cards.backEn: Although the ticket was expensive, the show was worth it.

cards.frontJa: ___ she was nervous, her presentation was clear.
cards.backEn: Although she was nervous, her presentation was clear.

cards.frontJa: ___ traffic was heavy, we arrived on time.
cards.backEn: Although traffic was heavy, we arrived on time.

cards.frontJa: ___ I disagree with the plan, I will support the team.
cards.backEn: Although I disagree with the plan, I will support the team.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 疲れていたが、最後まで参加した。
cards.backEn: Although I was tired, I stayed until the end.

cards.frontJa: ___ the data is incomplete, the trend looks positive.
cards.backEn: Although the data is incomplete, the trend looks positive.

cards.frontJa: ___ he apologized, some colleagues remain upset.
cards.backEn: Although he apologized, some colleagues remain upset.

cards.frontJa: ___ it's a small detail, it affects the whole design.
cards.backEn: Although it's a small detail, it affects the whole design.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 予算は限られているが、質は落としたくない。
cards.backEn: Although the budget is tight, we don't want to cut quality.

cards.frontJa: ___ we had practiced, the first question surprised us.
cards.backEn: Although we had practiced, the first question surprised us.

cards.frontJa: ___ the restaurant was busy, the service stayed friendly.
cards.backEn: Although the restaurant was busy, the service stayed friendly.

cards.frontJa: ___ I had sent a reminder, a few people forgot.
cards.backEn: Although I had sent a reminder, a few people forgot.

cards.frontJa: ___ the risk is low, we should document it.
cards.backEn: Although the risk is low, we should document it.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 年齢は若いが、とても責任感がある。
cards.backEn: Although she is young, she is very responsible.

cards.frontJa: ___ it sounds simple, implementation will take weeks.
cards.backEn: Although it sounds simple, implementation will take weeks.

cards.frontJa: ___ we lost the first game, we reached the final.
cards.backEn: Although we lost the first game, we reached the final.

cards.frontJa: ___ the museum was crowded, we enjoyed the exhibit.
cards.backEn: Although the museum was crowded, we enjoyed the exhibit.

cards.frontJa: ___ I had doubts, the results convinced me.
cards.backEn: Although I had doubts, the results convinced me.

cards.frontJa: ___ the flight was delayed, we made our connection.
cards.backEn: Although the flight was delayed, we made our connection.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 反対は多かったが、案は可決された。
cards.backEn: Although there was a lot of opposition, the proposal was approved.

cards.frontJa: ___ the software is old, it rarely crashes.
cards.backEn: Although the software is old, it rarely crashes.

cards.frontJa: ___ he works remotely, he attends every stand-up.
cards.backEn: Although he works remotely, he attends every stand-up.

cards.frontJa: ___ winter here is mild, we still get snow.
cards.backEn: Although winter here is mild, we still get snow.

cards.frontJa: ___ the sample is small, the pattern is clear.
cards.backEn: Although the sample is small, the pattern is clear.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 経験は浅いが、学ぶ意欲は高い。
cards.backEn: Although his experience is limited, he is eager to learn.

cards.frontJa: ___ the deadline moved, the scope did not shrink.
cards.backEn: Although the deadline moved, the scope did not shrink.

cards.frontJa: ___ she speaks softly, people listen carefully.
cards.backEn: Although she speaks softly, people listen carefully.

cards.frontJa: ___ costs rose, we stayed within the cap.
cards.backEn: Although costs rose, we stayed within the cap.

cards.frontJa: ___ I had warned them, they tried the shortcut.
cards.backEn: Although I had warned them, they tried the shortcut.

cards.frontJa: ___ I set two alarms, I overslept.
cards.backEn: Even though I set two alarms, I overslept.

cards.frontJa: ___ we rehearsed, I forgot my line on stage.
cards.backEn: Even though we rehearsed, I forgot my line on stage.

cards.frontJa: ___ it was a weekday, the beach was packed.
cards.backEn: Even though it was a weekday, the beach was packed.

cards.frontJa: ___ he studied hard, the exam felt unfair.
cards.backEn: Even though he studied hard, the exam felt unfair.

cards.frontJa: ___ she saved money, she couldn't afford the repair.
cards.backEn: Even though she saved money, she couldn't afford the repair.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 注意されたのに、同じミスをした。
cards.backEn: Even though I had been warned, I made the same mistake.

cards.frontJa: ___ we left early, traffic was still awful.
cards.backEn: Even though we left early, traffic was still awful.

cards.frontJa: ___ the instructions were clear, I assembled it wrong.
cards.backEn: Even though the instructions were clear, I assembled it wrong.

cards.frontJa: ___ they offered a discount, I didn't buy it.
cards.backEn: Even though they offered a discount, I didn't buy it.

cards.frontJa: ___ I locked the door, I worried all night.
cards.backEn: Even though I locked the door, I worried all night.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 証拠があったのに、信じてもらえなかった。
cards.backEn: Even though there was evidence, they didn't believe me.

cards.frontJa: ___ the path was steep, we reached the summit.
cards.backEn: Even though the path was steep, we reached the summit.

cards.frontJa: ___ I muted my mic, background noise leaked in.
cards.backEn: Even though I muted my mic, background noise leaked in.

cards.frontJa: ___ we doubled the staff, queues remained long.
cards.backEn: Even though we doubled the staff, queues remained long.

cards.frontJa: ___ he had a visa, border control questioned him.
cards.backEn: Even though he had a visa, border control questioned him.

cards.frontJa: ___ I don't drink coffee, I love the smell.
cards.backEn: Even though I don't drink coffee, I love the smell.

cards.frontJa: ___ the forecast was sunny, we packed rain gear.
cards.backEn: Even though the forecast was sunny, we packed rain gear.

cards.frontJa: ___ she had retired, she mentored new hires.
cards.backEn: Even though she had retired, she mentored new hires.

cards.frontJa: ___ the room was small, the acoustics were great.
cards.backEn: Even though the room was small, the acoustics were great.

cards.frontJa: ___ I had eaten, I was still hungry.
cards.backEn: Even though I had eaten, I was still hungry.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 忙しかったのに、手伝ってくれた。
cards.backEn: Even though she was busy, she helped me.

cards.frontJa: ___ we signed a contract, they changed the terms.
cards.backEn: Even though we signed a contract, they changed the terms.

cards.frontJa: ___ the movie got bad reviews, I enjoyed it.
cards.backEn: Even though the movie got bad reviews, I enjoyed it.

cards.frontJa: ___ he trains daily, injuries slowed him down.
cards.backEn: Even though he trains daily, injuries slowed him down.

cards.frontJa: ___ I explained twice, he looked confused.
cards.backEn: Even though I explained twice, he looked confused.

cards.frontJa: ___ the laptop is old, it runs smoothly.
cards.backEn: Even though the laptop is old, it runs smoothly.

cards.frontJa: ___ we lowered the price, sales stayed flat.
cards.backEn: Even though we lowered the price, sales stayed flat.

cards.frontJa: ___ she was a beginner, she finished the race.
cards.backEn: Even though she was a beginner, she finished the race.

cards.frontJa: ___ I turned notifications off, I checked my phone.
cards.backEn: Even though I turned notifications off, I checked my phone.

cards.frontJa: ___ winter had ended, snow fell in April.
cards.backEn: Even though winter had ended, snow fell in April.

cards.frontJa: ___ I respect his work, I can't agree with this decision.
cards.backEn: While I respect his work, I can't agree with this decision.

cards.frontJa: ___ the idea is creative, it isn't practical yet.
cards.backEn: While the idea is creative, it isn't practical yet.

cards.frontJa: ___ costs have fallen, quality has improved.
cards.backEn: While costs have fallen, quality has improved.

cards.frontJa: ___ some regions grew fast, others stagnated.
cards.backEn: While some regions grew fast, others stagnated.

cards.frontJa: ___ I understand the urgency, we need more data.
cards.backEn: While I understand the urgency, we need more data.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼は慎重だが、チームはスピードを求めている。
cards.backEn: While he is cautious, the team wants to move faster.

cards.frontJa: ___ the app is popular, support tickets keep rising.
cards.backEn: While the app is popular, support tickets keep rising.

cards.frontJa: ___ exports rose, domestic sales slipped.
cards.backEn: While exports rose, domestic sales slipped.

cards.frontJa: ___ I sympathize with them, policy won't change.
cards.backEn: While I sympathize with them, policy won't change.

cards.frontJa: ___ the headline looks scary, the risk is contained.
cards.backEn: While the headline looks scary, the risk is contained.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 若者は増えている一方で、高齢者も増えている。
cards.backEn: While the youth population is growing, the elderly population is growing too.

cards.frontJa: ___ we celebrate the win, we should review mistakes.
cards.backEn: While we celebrate the win, we should review mistakes.

cards.frontJa: ___ demand is strong, supply chains remain fragile.
cards.backEn: While demand is strong, supply chains remain fragile.

cards.frontJa: ___ she prefers text, clients want calls.
cards.backEn: While she prefers text, clients want calls.

cards.frontJa: ___ the city is expensive, it offers opportunities.
cards.backEn: While the city is expensive, it offers opportunities.

cards.frontJa: ___ I was cooking, the phone rang.
cards.backEn: While I was cooking, the phone rang.

cards.frontJa: ___ they were debating, I took notes.
cards.backEn: While they were debating, I took notes.

cards.frontJa: ___ we waited, the line barely moved.
cards.backEn: While we waited, the line barely moved.

cards.frontJa: ___ she presented, the slides advanced automatically.
cards.backEn: While she presented, the slides advanced automatically.

cards.frontJa: ___ I commuted, I listened to a podcast.
cards.backEn: While I commuted, I listened to a podcast.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼が話している間、皆が静かに聞いていた。
cards.backEn: While he was speaking, everyone listened quietly.

cards.frontJa: ___ the backup ran, we couldn't edit the file.
cards.backEn: While the backup ran, we couldn't edit the file.

cards.frontJa: ___ it was raining, joggers kept circling the park.
cards.backEn: While it was raining, joggers kept circling the park.

cards.frontJa: ___ you review this section, I'll handle the intro.
cards.backEn: While you review this section, I'll handle the intro.

cards.frontJa: ___ the band tuned, the crowd cheered.
cards.backEn: While the band tuned, the crowd cheered.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 工事が進む間、騒音が続いた。
cards.backEn: While construction continued, the noise went on.

cards.frontJa: ___ he slept, his phone charged.
cards.backEn: While he slept, his phone charged.

cards.frontJa: ___ we hiked, clouds rolled in.
cards.backEn: While we hiked, clouds rolled in.

cards.frontJa: ___ the video rendered, I grabbed lunch.
cards.backEn: While the video rendered, I grabbed lunch.

cards.frontJa: ___ they negotiated, lawyers reviewed the draft.
cards.backEn: While they negotiated, lawyers reviewed the draft.

cards.frontJa: If I ___ more free time, I would travel more.
cards.backEn: If I had more free time, I would travel more.

cards.frontJa: If she ___ here, she would love this neighborhood.
cards.backEn: If she lived here, she would love this neighborhood.

cards.frontJa: If we ___ a bigger budget, we could hire two designers.
cards.backEn: If we had a bigger budget, we could hire two designers.

cards.frontJa: If it ___ warmer, we could eat outside tonight.
cards.backEn: If it were warmer, we could eat outside tonight.

cards.frontJa: If I ___ you, I would ask for a second opinion.
cards.backEn: If I were you, I would ask for a second opinion.

cards.frontJa: Translate: もし私が社長なら、方針を変えるだろう。
cards.backEn: If I were the CEO, I would change the strategy.

cards.frontJa: If the server ___ faster, the app would feel smoother.
cards.backEn: If the server were faster, the app would feel smoother.

cards.frontJa: If he ___ the truth, he would feel relieved.
cards.backEn: If he knew the truth, he would feel relieved.

cards.frontJa: If we ___ another week, we could polish the report.
cards.backEn: If we had another week, we could polish the report.

cards.frontJa: If this ___ my project, I would simplify the scope.
cards.backEn: If this were my project, I would simplify the scope.

cards.frontJa: Translate: もし時間が逆戻りできるなら、あの日に戻りたい。
cards.backEn: If I could turn back time, I would return to that day.

cards.frontJa: If they ___ stricter rules, fewer mistakes would happen.
cards.backEn: If they had stricter rules, fewer mistakes would happen.

cards.frontJa: If I ___ fluent, I would apply for that role.
cards.backEn: If I were fluent, I would apply for that role.

cards.frontJa: If the venue ___ larger, we could invite everyone.
cards.backEn: If the venue were larger, we could invite everyone.

cards.frontJa: If we ___ unlimited storage, we wouldn't delete files.
cards.backEn: If we had unlimited storage, we wouldn't delete files.

cards.frontJa: If I ___ speak French, I could work in Montreal.
cards.backEn: If I could speak French, I could work in Montreal.

cards.frontJa: If she ___ join us, the discussion would be richer.
cards.backEn: If she could join us, the discussion would be richer.

cards.frontJa: If we ___ meet in person, I'd explain it better.
cards.backEn: If we could meet in person, I'd explain it better.

cards.frontJa: If nothing ___ in the way, we would launch in June.
cards.backEn: If nothing stood in the way, we would launch in June.

cards.frontJa: If I ___ one wish, I'd choose better work-life balance.
cards.backEn: If I had one wish, I'd choose better work-life balance.

cards.frontJa: Translate: もし雨が止めば、散歩に行くだろう。
cards.backEn: If the rain stopped, I would go for a walk.

cards.frontJa: If he ___ less shy, he would network more.
cards.backEn: If he were less shy, he would network more.

cards.frontJa: If the policy ___ different, morale might improve.
cards.backEn: If the policy were different, morale might improve.

cards.frontJa: If I ___ access to those logs, I could prove it.
cards.backEn: If I had access to those logs, I could prove it.

cards.frontJa: If it ___ not so late, I'd call her now.
cards.backEn: If it were not so late, I'd call her now.

cards.frontJa: Translate: もし君の立場なら、どうする？
cards.backEn: What would you do if you were in my position?

cards.frontJa: If we ___ a mentor, we would avoid these pitfalls.
cards.backEn: If we had a mentor, we would avoid these pitfalls.

cards.frontJa: If the data ___ public, trust would erode.
cards.backEn: If the data were public, trust would erode.

cards.frontJa: If I ___ younger, I'd learn the piano.
cards.backEn: If I were younger, I'd learn the piano.

cards.frontJa: If the board ___ fully aligned, decisions would be faster.
cards.backEn: If the board were fully aligned, decisions would be faster.

cards.frontJa: If I ___ managing this team, I would reset expectations.
cards.backEn: If I were managing this team, I would reset expectations.

cards.frontJa: If this ___ a client-facing bug, we would patch it tonight.
cards.backEn: If this were a client-facing bug, we would patch it tonight.

cards.frontJa: If we ___ starting over, I would pick a simpler stack.
cards.backEn: If we were starting over, I would pick a simpler stack.

cards.frontJa: If I ___ the customer, I would want clearer pricing.
cards.backEn: If I were the customer, I would want clearer pricing.

cards.frontJa: If ethics ___ optional, we would lose credibility.
cards.backEn: If ethics were optional, we would lose credibility.

cards.frontJa: Translate: もし私が裁判官なら、もっと厳しく罰する。
cards.backEn: If I were the judge, I would impose a stricter penalty.

cards.frontJa: If funding ___ guaranteed, we would expand the lab.
cards.backEn: If funding were guaranteed, we would expand the lab.

cards.frontJa: If silence ___ agreement, we would have a problem.
cards.backEn: If silence were agreement, we would have a problem.

cards.frontJa: If I ___ designing this flow, I would add a confirmation step.
cards.backEn: If I were designing this flow, I would add a confirmation step.

cards.frontJa: If reputation ___ currency, every reply would matter.
cards.backEn: If reputation were currency, every reply would matter.

cards.frontJa: Translate: もし資源が無限なら、実験をもっと重ねられる。
cards.backEn: If resources were unlimited, we could run more experiments.

cards.frontJa: If I ___ on the board, I would ask tougher questions.
cards.backEn: If I were on the board, I would ask tougher questions.

cards.frontJa: If empathy ___ standard here, feedback would sting less.
cards.backEn: If empathy were standard here, feedback would sting less.

cards.frontJa: If speed ___ the only metric, quality would suffer.
cards.backEn: If speed were the only metric, quality would suffer.

cards.frontJa: If I ___ rewriting this email, I would shorten the opening.
cards.backEn: If I were rewriting this email, I would shorten the opening.

cards.frontJa: I ___ that remote work boosts productivity for many roles.
cards.backEn: I believe that remote work boosts productivity for many roles.

cards.frontJa: I ___ that early feedback saves money later.
cards.backEn: I believe that early feedback saves money later.

cards.frontJa: I ___ that everyone deserves a clear career path.
cards.backEn: I believe that everyone deserves a clear career path.

cards.frontJa: I ___ that small habits shape long-term health.
cards.backEn: I believe that small habits shape long-term health.

cards.frontJa: I ___ that transparency builds trust on teams.
cards.backEn: I believe that transparency builds trust on teams.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 公平な手続きが正義だと信じている。
cards.backEn: I believe that fair procedures are what justice means.

cards.frontJa: I ___ that we should measure outcomes, not hours.
cards.backEn: I believe that we should measure outcomes, not hours.

cards.frontJa: I ___ that language learning takes patient practice.
cards.backEn: I believe that language learning takes patient practice.

cards.frontJa: I ___ that cities need greener public spaces.
cards.backEn: I believe that cities need greener public spaces.

cards.frontJa: I ___ that good design is invisible until it breaks.
cards.backEn: I believe that good design is invisible until it breaks.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 多様性は創造性を高めると信じている。
cards.backEn: I believe that diversity strengthens creativity.

cards.frontJa: I ___ that we owe users honest release notes.
cards.backEn: I believe that we owe users honest release notes.

cards.frontJa: I ___ that sleep is undervalued in startup culture.
cards.backEn: I believe that sleep is undervalued in startup culture.

cards.frontJa: I ___ that mentors accelerate growth.
cards.backEn: I believe that mentors accelerate growth.

cards.frontJa: I ___ that curiosity matters more than raw talent.
cards.backEn: I believe that curiosity matters more than raw talent.

cards.frontJa: ___ my view, the benefits outweigh the risks.
cards.backEn: In my view, the benefits outweigh the risks.

cards.frontJa: Personally, I ___ we should delay the launch.
cards.backEn: Personally, I think we should delay the launch.

cards.frontJa: ___ speaking, the timeline looks unrealistic.
cards.backEn: Frankly speaking, the timeline looks unrealistic.

cards.frontJa: ___ I'm concerned about scope creep.
cards.backEn: To be honest, I'm concerned about scope creep.

cards.frontJa: ___ the evidence suggests we should wait.
cards.backEn: From my perspective, the evidence suggests we should wait.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 私の経験では、早すぎる最適化は危険だ。
cards.backEn: In my experience, premature optimization is risky.

cards.frontJa: ___ I would argue that consistency beats novelty here.
cards.backEn: Broadly, I would argue that consistency beats novelty here.

cards.frontJa: ___ it seems to me that we lack a single owner.
cards.backEn: It seems to me that we lack a single owner.

cards.frontJa: ___ I tend to think smaller teams ship faster.
cards.backEn: I tend to think smaller teams ship faster.

cards.frontJa: ___ I'm inclined to support the pilot project.
cards.backEn: I'm inclined to support the pilot project.

cards.frontJa: Translate: どちらかというと、慎重な方がいい。
cards.backEn: If anything, I'd rather be cautious.

cards.frontJa: ___ on balance, I'd choose the safer option.
cards.backEn: On balance, I'd choose the safer option.

cards.frontJa: ___ as I see it, communication is the bottleneck.
cards.backEn: As I see it, communication is the bottleneck.

cards.frontJa: ___ my impression is that morale has improved.
cards.backEn: My impression is that morale has improved.

cards.frontJa: ___ , we should pause the rollout until QA signs off.
cards.backEn: In my opinion, we should pause the rollout until QA signs off.

cards.frontJa: My ___ position is that we need written criteria.
cards.backEn: My firm position is that we need written criteria.

cards.frontJa: The ___ point I'm making is that cost isn't the only factor.
cards.backEn: The key point I'm making is that cost isn't the only factor.

cards.frontJa: Let me ___ my reasoning in three short bullets.
cards.backEn: Let me outline my reasoning in three short bullets.

cards.frontJa: I want to ___ this clearly: we can't accept further delay.
cards.backEn: I want to state this clearly: we can't accept further delay.

cards.frontJa: To ___ my argument, consider what happened last quarter.
cards.backEn: To strengthen my argument, consider what happened last quarter.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 結論として、反対せざるを得ない。
cards.backEn: In conclusion, I feel compelled to disagree.

cards.frontJa: What I'm ___ is that trust takes years to rebuild.
cards.backEn: What I'm driving at is that trust takes years to rebuild.

cards.frontJa: I should ___ that I don't speak for the whole department.
cards.backEn: I should stress that I don't speak for the whole department.

cards.frontJa: Another ___ worth highlighting is data privacy.
cards.backEn: Another angle worth highlighting is data privacy.

cards.frontJa: I am ___ convinced that this vendor is the right fit.
cards.backEn: I am strongly convinced that this vendor is the right fit.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 簡単に言えば、今はリスクが高すぎる。
cards.backEn: Put simply, the risk is too high right now.

cards.frontJa: If I may ___, this plan ignores frontline feedback.
cards.backEn: If I may be blunt, this plan ignores frontline feedback.

cards.frontJa: The ___ I want to leave you with is urgency.
cards.backEn: The takeaway I want to leave you with is urgency.

cards.frontJa: I am ___ opposed to cutting training first.
cards.backEn: I am firmly opposed to cutting training first.

cards.frontJa: Let me ___ one last example from our pilot.
cards.backEn: Let me cite one last example from our pilot.

cards.frontJa: I ___ with you that the first draft was weak.
cards.backEn: I agree with you that the first draft was weak.

cards.frontJa: I ___ — the timeline does look tight.
cards.backEn: I agree — the timeline does look tight.

cards.frontJa: I'm ___ on board with the safety checklist idea.
cards.backEn: I'm completely on board with the safety checklist idea.

cards.frontJa: I ___ partly: the design is elegant, but it's hard to maintain.
cards.backEn: I agree partly: the design is elegant, but it's hard to maintain.

cards.frontJa: You have a ___ there about vendor lock-in.
cards.backEn: You have a point there about vendor lock-in.

cards.frontJa: Translate: その通りだと思う。特にコストの面で。
cards.backEn: I think you're right, especially about the cost.

cards.frontJa: I couldn't ___ more about the need for documentation.
cards.backEn: I couldn't agree more about the need for documentation.

cards.frontJa: That's a ___ observation about user fatigue.
cards.backEn: That's a fair observation about user fatigue.

cards.frontJa: I'm ___ to agree if we add a rollback plan.
cards.backEn: I'm willing to agree if we add a rollback plan.

cards.frontJa: I see ___ you're coming from on staffing.
cards.backEn: I see where you're coming from on staffing.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 基本的には賛成だが、細部は詰めたい。
cards.backEn: I agree in principle, but I'd like to nail down the details.

cards.frontJa: I share your ___ about long-term sustainability.
cards.backEn: I share your concern about long-term sustainability.

cards.frontJa: I'm ___ with the direction, not every tactic.
cards.backEn: I'm aligned with the direction, not every tactic.

cards.frontJa: True, ___ we should still test edge cases.
cards.backEn: True, though we should still test edge cases.

cards.frontJa: I accept that ___ if we cap the budget.
cards.backEn: I accept that proposal if we cap the budget.

cards.frontJa: I'm ___ sure I agree with that conclusion.
cards.backEn: I'm not sure I agree with that conclusion.

cards.frontJa: I see it ___ — the risk profile looks different to me.
cards.backEn: I see it differently — the risk profile looks different to me.

cards.frontJa: I have ___ about relying on a single supplier.
cards.backEn: I have reservations about relying on a single supplier.

cards.frontJa: I'd ___ to push back gently on that assumption.
cards.backEn: I'd like to push back gently on that assumption.

cards.frontJa: I'm ___ I can't support cutting QA hours.
cards.backEn: I'm afraid I can't support cutting QA hours.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 完全には同意できない。
cards.backEn: I don't entirely agree with that.

cards.frontJa: With respect, I ___ that the data supports the claim.
cards.backEn: With respect, I don't think the data supports the claim.

cards.frontJa: I may be ___, but this feels rushed.
cards.backEn: I may be wrong, but this feels rushed.

cards.frontJa: I'm ___ on the fence until we see the prototype.
cards.backEn: I'm still on the fence until we see the prototype.

cards.frontJa: I wouldn't go ___ far as to call it a failure.
cards.backEn: I wouldn't go so far as to call it a failure.

cards.frontJa: Translate: その解釈には疑問がある。
cards.backEn: I'm skeptical of that interpretation.

cards.frontJa: I hear you, ___ I'm not fully convinced yet.
cards.backEn: I hear you, but I'm not fully convinced yet.

cards.frontJa: Could we ___ that from another angle?
cards.backEn: Could we look at that from another angle?

cards.frontJa: I need more ___ before I can endorse this.
cards.backEn: I need more evidence before I can endorse this.

cards.frontJa: Let's agree to ___ — we'll test both hypotheses.
cards.backEn: Let's agree to disagree — we'll test both hypotheses.

cards.frontJa: We postponed the release ___ several critical bugs remained.
cards.backEn: We postponed the release because several critical bugs remained.

cards.frontJa: Morale improved ___ managers started listening in one-on-ones.
cards.backEn: Morale improved because managers started listening in one-on-ones.

cards.frontJa: Sales rose ___ the campaign targeted a younger audience.
cards.backEn: Sales rose because the campaign targeted a younger audience.

cards.frontJa: I was late ___ a signal failure stopped the trains.
cards.backEn: I was late because a signal failure stopped the trains.

cards.frontJa: We chose this vendor ___ their support response time is fast.
cards.backEn: We chose this vendor because their support response time is fast.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼は辞めた。待遇に不満があったからだ。
cards.backEn: He quit because he was unhappy with the compensation.

cards.frontJa: The event succeeded ___ volunteers coordinated every detail.
cards.backEn: The event succeeded because volunteers coordinated every detail.

cards.frontJa: I trust her ___ she admits mistakes quickly.
cards.backEn: I trust her because she admits mistakes quickly.

cards.frontJa: Traffic was awful ___ three lanes were closed for repair.
cards.backEn: Traffic was awful because three lanes were closed for repair.

cards.frontJa: We invested ___ we believed the market would mature.
cards.backEn: We invested because we believed the market would mature.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 結果が良かったのは、準備が徹底していたからだ。
cards.backEn: The outcome was good because preparation was thorough.

cards.frontJa: I declined ___ I already committed to another project.
cards.backEn: I declined because I already committed to another project.

cards.frontJa: The app crashed ___ memory usage spiked unexpectedly.
cards.backEn: The app crashed because memory usage spiked unexpectedly.

cards.frontJa: She stayed calm ___ she had rehearsed worst-case scenarios.
cards.backEn: She stayed calm because she had rehearsed worst-case scenarios.

cards.frontJa: We expanded ___ customer demand exceeded forecasts.
cards.backEn: We expanded because customer demand exceeded forecasts.

decks.titleJa: 原因と結果② therefore / as a result
decks.titleEn: Cause & effect — therefore & as a result

cards.frontJa: Costs rose sharply. ___, we froze hiring.
cards.backEn: Costs rose sharply. Therefore, we froze hiring.

cards.frontJa: The trial failed. ___, we pivoted to a new model.
cards.backEn: The trial failed. As a result, we pivoted to a new model.

cards.frontJa: Demand doubled. ___, lead times stretched.
cards.backEn: Demand doubled. Consequently, lead times stretched.

cards.frontJa: We missed two milestones. ___, stakeholders lost confidence.
cards.backEn: We missed two milestones. As a result, stakeholders lost confidence.

cards.frontJa: The policy changed. ___, we updated the handbook.
cards.backEn: The policy changed. Therefore, we updated the handbook.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 品質が上がった。その結果、返品が減った。
cards.backEn: Quality improved. As a result, returns decreased.

cards.frontJa: The server overheated. ___, we added cooling fans.
cards.backEn: The server overheated. Therefore, we added cooling fans.

cards.frontJa: Users complained about clutter. ___, we simplified the UI.
cards.backEn: Users complained about clutter. Consequently, we simplified the UI.

cards.frontJa: Funding arrived early. ___, we accelerated the roadmap.
cards.backEn: Funding arrived early. As a result, we accelerated the roadmap.

cards.frontJa: The keynote ran long. ___, lunch started late.
cards.backEn: The keynote ran long. Therefore, lunch started late.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 交渉が決裂した。その結果、訴訟になった。
cards.backEn: Negotiations collapsed. As a result, it went to court.

cards.frontJa: We lacked data. ___, the report stayed qualitative.
cards.backEn: We lacked data. Therefore, the report stayed qualitative.

cards.frontJa: Attendance dropped. ___, we merged two sessions.
cards.backEn: Attendance dropped. As a result, we merged two sessions.

cards.frontJa: The storm passed. ___, flights resumed by evening.
cards.backEn: The storm passed. Consequently, flights resumed by evening.

cards.frontJa: Trust eroded. ___, turnover increased.
cards.backEn: Trust eroded. As a result, turnover increased.

decks.titleJa: 原因と結果③ つなぎ

cards.frontJa: Because we ___ early, we caught the mistake before launch.
cards.backEn: Because we tested early, we caught the mistake before launch.

cards.frontJa: Since the ___ was unclear, two teams duplicated work.
cards.backEn: Since the ownership was unclear, two teams duplicated work.

cards.frontJa: Given the ___, we recommended a phased rollout.
cards.backEn: Given the uncertainty, we recommended a phased rollout.

cards.frontJa: Owing to ___ cuts, the library reduced its hours.
cards.backEn: Owing to budget cuts, the library reduced its hours.

cards.frontJa: Due to ___ weather, the outdoor class moved online.
cards.backEn: Due to severe weather, the outdoor class moved online.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 人手不足のため、営業時間を短縮した。
cards.backEn: Because of a staff shortage, we shortened opening hours.

cards.frontJa: Thanks to ___ coaching, her confidence grew.
cards.backEn: Thanks to patient coaching, her confidence grew.

cards.frontJa: The delay stemmed ___ a mislabeled shipment.
cards.backEn: The delay stemmed from a mislabeled shipment.

cards.frontJa: The outage was ___ human error, not a cyberattack.
cards.backEn: The outage was caused by human error, not a cyberattack.

cards.frontJa: One slip led ___ another, and the deadline slipped.
cards.backEn: One slip led to another, and the deadline slipped.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 連鎖的に、コストが膨らんだ。
cards.backEn: Costs ballooned in a chain reaction.

cards.frontJa: Poor sleep contributed ___ several mistakes that week.
cards.backEn: Poor sleep contributed to several mistakes that week.

cards.frontJa: The spike in demand triggered ___ hiring freeze to lift.
cards.backEn: The spike in demand triggered the hiring freeze to lift.

cards.frontJa: A single bug cascaded ___ multiple customer complaints.
cards.backEn: A single bug cascaded into multiple customer complaints.

cards.frontJa: Their silence implied ___ they disagreed with the plan.
cards.backEn: Their silence implied that they disagreed with the plan.

cards.frontJa: I felt ___ when they announced my name.
cards.backEn: I felt overwhelmed when they announced my name.

cards.frontJa: I was ___ relieved once the results came back negative.
cards.backEn: I was incredibly relieved once the results came back negative.

cards.frontJa: The news left me ___ for days.
cards.backEn: The news left me anxious for days.

cards.frontJa: I was ___ proud watching my teammate present.
cards.backEn: I was genuinely proud watching my teammate present.

cards.frontJa: The crowd's energy made me ___ alive.
cards.backEn: The crowd's energy made me feel alive.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 寂しさと安堵が入り混じった。
cards.backEn: I felt a mix of loneliness and relief.

cards.frontJa: I walked out feeling ___ about the whole process.
cards.backEn: I walked out feeling bitter about the whole process.

cards.frontJa: Her kindness touched me ___ than I expected.
cards.backEn: Her kindness touched me more deeply than I expected.

cards.frontJa: I was ___ embarrassed when I mispronounced his name.
cards.backEn: I was slightly embarrassed when I mispronounced his name.

cards.frontJa: The silence in the room felt ___ .
cards.backEn: The silence in the room felt awkward.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 達成感で胸がいっぱいだった。
cards.backEn: I felt a huge sense of accomplishment.

cards.frontJa: I stayed calm, ___ I was shaking inside.
cards.backEn: I stayed calm, though I was shaking inside.

cards.frontJa: The ending moved me ___ I cried in the theater.
cards.backEn: The ending moved me so much that I cried in the theater.

cards.frontJa: I felt ___ out after three weeks of night shifts.
cards.backEn: I felt burned out after three weeks of night shifts.

cards.frontJa: Hearing their laughter made the trip ___ worthwhile.
cards.backEn: Hearing their laughter made the trip feel worthwhile.

cards.frontJa: The alley smelled ___ rain and fried garlic.
cards.backEn: The alley smelled of rain and fried garlic.

cards.frontJa: We heard distant ___ until midnight.
cards.backEn: We heard distant drums until midnight.

cards.frontJa: Sunlight hit the water ___ it looked like glass.
cards.backEn: Sunlight hit the water so that it looked like glass.

cards.frontJa: I remember how ___ the stone steps felt under my shoes.
cards.backEn: I remember how uneven the stone steps felt under my shoes.

cards.frontJa: Looking back, I ___ how naive I was.
cards.backEn: Looking back, I realize how naive I was.

cards.frontJa: Translate: あの瞬間、時間が止まったように感じた。
cards.backEn: In that moment, it felt as if time had stopped.

cards.frontJa: What stuck ___ me was the guide's patience.
cards.backEn: What stuck with me was the guide's patience.

cards.frontJa: In hindsight, we ___ have booked earlier.
cards.backEn: In hindsight, we should have booked earlier.

cards.frontJa: The market was ___ that we could barely move.
cards.backEn: The market was so crowded that we could barely move.

cards.frontJa: I noticed ___ detail: every window was a different color.
cards.backEn: I noticed a strange detail: every window was a different color.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 写真では伝わらない空気があった。
cards.backEn: There was an atmosphere that photos couldn't capture.

cards.frontJa: We stayed long enough ___ the lights dimmed.
cards.backEn: We stayed long enough to watch the lights dim.

cards.frontJa: The hike was harder ___ we imagined.
cards.backEn: The hike was harder than we imagined.

cards.frontJa: I can still ___ the taste of that soup.
cards.backEn: I can still recall the taste of that soup.

cards.frontJa: That trip changed ___ I think about travel.
cards.backEn: That trip changed how I think about travel.

cards.frontJa: On the ___ hand, remote work saves commute time.
cards.backEn: On the one hand, remote work saves commute time.

cards.frontJa: On the ___ hand, collaboration can suffer.
cards.backEn: On the other hand, collaboration can suffer.

cards.frontJa: One ___ is lower office rent.
cards.backEn: One advantage is lower office rent.

cards.frontJa: One ___ is weaker team bonding.
cards.backEn: One drawback is weaker team bonding.

cards.frontJa: Let's ___ the pros and cons before we decide.
cards.backEn: Let's weigh the pros and cons before we decide.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 賛成派と反対派の両方を聞こう。
cards.backEn: Let's hear both sides of the argument.

cards.frontJa: A ___ argument for the merger is scale.
cards.backEn: A strong argument for the merger is scale.

cards.frontJa: A ___ argument against it is cultural clash.
cards.backEn: A common argument against it is cultural clash.

cards.frontJa: We should ___ counterarguments in the appendix.
cards.backEn: We should list counterarguments in the appendix.

cards.frontJa: I'm playing ___ advocate for a moment.
cards.backEn: I'm playing devil's advocate for a moment.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 反対意見も真剣に検討すべきだ。
cards.backEn: We should take opposing views seriously.

cards.frontJa: The trade-off ___ speed versus quality is real.
cards.backEn: The trade-off between speed versus quality is real.

cards.frontJa: If we ___ only upside, we'll be blindsided.
cards.backEn: If we consider only upside, we'll be blindsided.

cards.frontJa: Let's ___ the discussion to three key risks.
cards.backEn: Let's narrow the discussion to three key risks.

cards.frontJa: We need a ___ summary for leadership.
cards.backEn: We need a balanced summary for leadership.

cards.frontJa: That objection ___ some weight, but it isn't decisive.
cards.backEn: That objection carries some weight, but it isn't decisive.

cards.frontJa: I grant you that ___, yet the trend still worries me.
cards.backEn: I grant you that profits rose, yet the trend still worries me.

cards.frontJa: Even if we accept ___, the conclusion doesn't follow.
cards.backEn: Even if we accept their premise, the conclusion doesn't follow.

cards.frontJa: That rebuttal ___ the weakest part of their case.
cards.backEn: That rebuttal targets the weakest part of their case.

cards.frontJa: We should ___ their assumptions, not just their conclusions.
cards.backEn: We should challenge their assumptions, not just their conclusions.

cards.frontJa: Translate: データの解釈が争点だ。
cards.backEn: The dispute is over how to interpret the data.

cards.frontJa: Their analogy ___ because the contexts differ.
cards.backEn: Their analogy breaks down because the contexts differ.

cards.frontJa: I'd ___ that with data from our own pilot.
cards.backEn: I'd counter that with data from our own pilot.

cards.frontJa: Let's not ___ the ethical dimension.
cards.backEn: Let's not overlook the ethical dimension.

cards.frontJa: The burden of ___ should be on whoever proposes change.
cards.backEn: The burden of proof should be on whoever proposes change.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 感情的な論点に逃げないでほしい。
cards.backEn: Please don't retreat into emotional arguments.

cards.frontJa: We can ___ their claim without attacking them personally.
cards.backEn: We can refute their claim without attacking them personally.

cards.frontJa: A cooler ___ would be to run a controlled experiment.
cards.backEn: A cooler response would be to run a controlled experiment.

cards.frontJa: Let's ___ where we agree before we fight over details.
cards.backEn: Let's map where we agree before we fight over details.

cards.frontJa: I'm trying to ___ the strongest version of their view.
cards.backEn: I'm trying to steelman the strongest version of their view.

decks.titleJa: 職場① アイデア提示

cards.frontJa: I'd ___ to frame the problem in three layers.
cards.backEn: I'd like to frame the problem in three layers.

cards.frontJa: ___ I'd suggest we pilot this in one region first.
cards.backEn: Broadly, I'd suggest we pilot this in one region first.

cards.frontJa: The ___ I'd like to make is about scalability.
cards.backEn: The core point I'd like to make is about scalability.

cards.frontJa: Let me ___ this with a simple diagram.
cards.backEn: Let me illustrate this with a simple diagram.

cards.frontJa: If we ___ the question, we avoid scope creep.
cards.backEn: If we narrow the question, we avoid scope creep.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 結論から言うと、今は投資の時だ。
cards.backEn: Bottom line: now is the time to invest.

cards.frontJa: I'd ___ the floor to our analyst for the numbers.
cards.backEn: I'd yield the floor to our analyst for the numbers.

cards.frontJa: Could I get two ___ on this slide?
cards.backEn: Could I get two minutes on this slide?

cards.frontJa: I'll ___ my case in four bullets.
cards.backEn: I'll state my case in four bullets.

cards.frontJa: What I'm ___ is a phased rollout, not a big bang.
cards.backEn: What I'm proposing is a phased rollout, not a big bang.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 前提を共有してもいいですか。
cards.backEn: May I share a few assumptions up front?

cards.frontJa: Let me ___ why this matters to finance.
cards.backEn: Let me spell out why this matters to finance.

cards.frontJa: I'd welcome ___ on whether this timeline is sane.
cards.backEn: I'd welcome pushback on whether this timeline is sane.

cards.frontJa: To ___ expectations, this won't fix everything overnight.
cards.backEn: To set expectations, this won't fix everything overnight.

cards.frontJa: I'll close ___ a single ask of the leadership team.
cards.backEn: I'll close with a single ask of the leadership team.

decks.titleJa: 職場② 発表のつなぎ

cards.frontJa: ___ I'll outline the background.
cards.backEn: First, I'll outline the background.

cards.frontJa: ___ I'll walk through the methodology.
cards.backEn: Next, I'll walk through the methodology.

cards.frontJa: ___ I'll share results and limitations.
cards.backEn: Then I'll share results and limitations.

cards.frontJa: ___ I'll open the floor for questions.
cards.backEn: Finally, I'll open the floor for questions.

cards.frontJa: ___ another angle, consider the regulatory view.
cards.backEn: From another angle, consider the regulatory view.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 話を戻すが、先ほどの数字についてだ。
cards.backEn: To return to an earlier point about those figures.

cards.frontJa: ___ deeper, the root cause is coordination.
cards.backEn: Digging deeper, the root cause is coordination.

cards.frontJa: ___ briefly, we underestimated integration work.
cards.backEn: To recap briefly, we underestimated integration work.

cards.frontJa: ___ forward, we will publish weekly metrics.
cards.backEn: Going forward, we will publish weekly metrics.

cards.frontJa: ___ contrast, last year's curve was flat.
cards.backEn: By contrast, last year's curve was flat.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 具体例を一つ挙げると…
cards.backEn: To give one concrete example…

cards.frontJa: ___ aside, the culture fit looks strong.
cards.backEn: Joking aside, the culture fit looks strong.

cards.frontJa: ___ up, three teams need clearer owners.
cards.backEn: Wrapping up, three teams need clearer owners.

cards.frontJa: ___ the interest of time, I'll skip the appendix.
cards.backEn: In the interest of time, I'll skip the appendix.

cards.frontJa: ___ the record, I support the recommendation.
cards.backEn: For the record, I support the recommendation.

decks.titleJa: 職場③ アップデート

cards.frontJa: Quick ___ : we shipped the hotfix last night.
cards.backEn: Quick update: we shipped the hotfix last night.

cards.frontJa: We're ___ track to finish the redesign by Friday.
cards.backEn: We're on track to finish the redesign by Friday.

cards.frontJa: We're slightly ___ because of a vendor delay.
cards.backEn: We're slightly behind because of a vendor delay.

cards.frontJa: No ___ this week — same blockers as last week.
cards.backEn: No major changes this week — same blockers as last week.

cards.frontJa: Risk ___ : medium, mainly integration.
cards.backEn: Risk level: medium, mainly integration.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 進捗は順調だが、人手が足りない。
cards.backEn: Progress is good, but we're short on people.

cards.frontJa: I'll ___ the dashboard link after this call.
cards.backEn: I'll paste the dashboard link after this call.

cards.frontJa: Next ___ : finalize copy and run A/B tests.
cards.backEn: Next steps: finalize copy and run A/B tests.

cards.frontJa: ETA for the beta is ___, barring surprises.
cards.backEn: ETA for the beta is March 3, barring surprises.

cards.frontJa: We ___ a dependency on the legal review.
cards.backEn: We hit a dependency on the legal review.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 顧客からのフィードバックをまとめた。
cards.backEn: I've summarized feedback from customers.

cards.frontJa: I'll ___ the team by email with the new timeline.
cards.backEn: I'll brief the team by email with the new timeline.

cards.frontJa: Nothing ___ to report on security incidents.
cards.backEn: Nothing new to report on security incidents.

cards.frontJa: We're green ___ performance; yellow on staffing.
cards.backEn: We're green on performance; yellow on staffing.

cards.frontJa: I'll ___ a written summary to the channel.
cards.backEn: I'll post a written summary to the channel.

decks.titleJa: 職場④ アップデート②

cards.frontJa: Since we last met, we ___ two critical bugs.
cards.backEn: Since we last met, we closed two critical bugs.

cards.frontJa: We ___ scope slightly to protect the deadline.
cards.backEn: We reduced scope slightly to protect the deadline.

cards.frontJa: Adoption is up ___ week over week.
cards.backEn: Adoption is up 8% week over week.

cards.frontJa: Churn ticked ___ after the price change.
cards.backEn: Churn ticked upward after the price change.

cards.frontJa: We ___ a hiring freeze until Q3.
cards.backEn: We instituted a hiring freeze until Q3.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 予算は計画の92%まで消化した。
cards.backEn: We've used 92% of the planned budget.

cards.frontJa: Partnerships: one LOI ___, two in negotiation.
cards.backEn: Partnerships: one LOI signed, two in negotiation.

cards.frontJa: I'll ___ a risk register by EOD.
cards.backEn: I'll circulate a risk register by EOD.

cards.frontJa: We need a ___ on the API outage root cause.
cards.backEn: We need a decision on the API outage root cause.

cards.frontJa: Support backlog ___ from 40 to 22 tickets.
cards.backEn: Support backlog dropped from 40 to 22 tickets.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 来週はデモとレビューに集中する。
cards.backEn: Next week we'll focus on demos and reviews.

cards.frontJa: I'll ___ stakeholders once finance signs off.
cards.backEn: I'll notify stakeholders once finance signs off.

cards.frontJa: We ___ the launch by one sprint to harden security.
cards.backEn: We delayed the launch by one sprint to harden security.

cards.frontJa: Quality metrics are ___ except for mobile Safari.
cards.backEn: Quality metrics are stable except for mobile Safari.

cards.frontJa: I'll ___ a live demo in tomorrow's stand-up.
cards.backEn: I'll schedule a live demo in tomorrow's stand-up.

cards.frontJa: The ___ issue is inconsistent handoffs between teams.
cards.backEn: The core issue is inconsistent handoffs between teams.

cards.frontJa: We're seeing a ___ in communication after the reorg.
cards.backEn: We're seeing a pattern in communication after the reorg.

cards.frontJa: The ___ cause looks like a race condition.
cards.backEn: The probable cause looks like a race condition.

cards.frontJa: Symptoms ___ slow sync and duplicate records.
cards.backEn: Symptoms include slow sync and duplicate records.

cards.frontJa: We should ___ whether incentives are misaligned.
cards.backEn: We should examine whether incentives are misaligned.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 根本原因はプロセスの欠陥だと思う。
cards.backEn: I think the root cause is a flawed process.

cards.frontJa: Let's ___ the problem statement before we brainstorm fixes.
cards.backEn: Let's align on the problem statement before we brainstorm fixes.

cards.frontJa: The pain ___ mostly affects enterprise customers.
cards.backEn: The pain point mostly affects enterprise customers.

cards.frontJa: We lack ___ visibility into partner SLAs.
cards.backEn: We lack clear visibility into partner SLAs.

cards.frontJa: This isn't a one-off; it's a ___ failure.
cards.backEn: This isn't a one-off; it's a systemic failure.

cards.frontJa: Translate: リスクを過小評価してきた。
cards.backEn: We've been underestimating the risk.

cards.frontJa: The bottleneck ___ between design and engineering.
cards.backEn: The bottleneck sits between design and engineering.

cards.frontJa: We should ___ how often this error reproduces.
cards.backEn: We should quantify how often this error reproduces.

cards.frontJa: Stakeholders ___ frustrated with opaque status updates.
cards.backEn: Stakeholders feel frustrated with opaque status updates.

cards.frontJa: Let's ___ the scope of 'done' for this milestone.
cards.backEn: Let's define the scope of 'done' for this milestone.

cards.frontJa: One option ___ to split the monolith gradually.
cards.backEn: One option is to split the monolith gradually.

cards.frontJa: We could ___ a rotating on-call roster.
cards.backEn: We could pilot a rotating on-call roster.

cards.frontJa: I recommend we ___ a single source of truth for specs.
cards.backEn: I recommend we establish a single source of truth for specs.

cards.frontJa: Let's ___ owners for each workstream by Friday.
cards.backEn: Let's assign owners for each workstream by Friday.

cards.frontJa: We should ___ a blameless postmortem next week.
cards.backEn: We should schedule a blameless postmortem next week.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 短期的にはワークラウンドを、中長期では修正を。
cards.backEn: Short term: a workaround; long term: a proper fix.

cards.frontJa: Mitigation ___ include rate limits and caching.
cards.backEn: Mitigation could include rate limits and caching.

cards.frontJa: We might ___ external auditors if trust is low.
cards.backEn: We might bring in external auditors if trust is low.

cards.frontJa: Let's ___ a rollback plan before we flip the flag.
cards.backEn: Let's document a rollback plan before we flip the flag.

cards.frontJa: I'd ___ weekly syncs until the metrics stabilize.
cards.backEn: I'd propose weekly syncs until the metrics stabilize.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 成功指標を合意してから進めよう。
cards.backEn: Let's agree on success metrics before we proceed.

cards.frontJa: We should ___ user research before redesigning checkout.
cards.backEn: We should fund user research before redesigning checkout.

cards.frontJa: A quick win ___ to fix the broken signup link.
cards.backEn: A quick win is to fix the broken signup link.

cards.frontJa: Longer term, we should ___ toward modular services.
cards.backEn: Longer term, we should migrate toward modular services.

cards.frontJa: Let's ___ progress against these three KPIs.
cards.backEn: Let's track progress against these three KPIs.

cards.frontJa: ___ cultures, direct feedback can sound rude.
cards.backEn: Across cultures, direct feedback can sound rude.

cards.frontJa: In some places, ___ late is more acceptable than elsewhere.
cards.backEn: In some places, arriving late is more acceptable than elsewhere.

cards.frontJa: Food rituals often ___ family bonds.
cards.backEn: Food rituals often reflect family bonds.

cards.frontJa: ___ habits like bowing or handshakes carry social meaning.
cards.backEn: Small habits like bowing or handshakes carry social meaning.

cards.frontJa: Lifestyle ___ in cities versus countryside are widening.
cards.backEn: Lifestyle gaps in cities versus countryside are widening.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 宗教的な配慮を忘れないでほしい。
cards.backEn: Please remember to respect religious considerations.

cards.frontJa: Public ___ expectations differ from private norms.
cards.backEn: Public behavior expectations differ from private norms.

cards.frontJa: Gift-giving rules can ___ surprisingly formal.
cards.backEn: Gift-giving rules can feel surprisingly formal.

cards.frontJa: ___ media shapes how teens see body image.
cards.backEn: Social media shapes how teens see body image.

cards.frontJa: Work-life ___ means different things in different countries.
cards.backEn: Work-life balance means different things in different countries.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 「個人主義」は単純化しすぎだ。
cards.backEn: Calling it individualism is an oversimplification.

cards.frontJa: Festivals reveal ___ values a community celebrates.
cards.backEn: Festivals reveal which values a community celebrates.

cards.frontJa: Immigration debates often ___ fears about identity.
cards.backEn: Immigration debates often touch fears about identity.

cards.frontJa: ___ etiquette around shoes indoors trips visitors up.
cards.backEn: Simple etiquette around shoes indoors trips visitors up.

cards.frontJa: Cultural ___ isn't the same as stereotyping.
cards.backEn: Cultural awareness isn't the same as stereotyping.

cards.frontJa: ___ abroad taught me to question my defaults.
cards.backEn: Living abroad taught me to question my defaults.

cards.frontJa: I learned not to ___ silence as agreement.
cards.backEn: I learned not to read silence as agreement.

cards.frontJa: Humor doesn't always ___ borders cleanly.
cards.backEn: Humor doesn't always translate across borders cleanly.

cards.frontJa: Different generations ___ privacy online differently.
cards.backEn: Different generations view privacy online differently.

cards.frontJa: School lunches ___ class and health inequalities.
cards.backEn: School lunches mirror class and health inequalities.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 外食文化は都市と地方で違う。
cards.backEn: Eating-out culture differs between urban and rural areas.

cards.frontJa: ___ norms around tipping confuse many travelers.
cards.backEn: Unwritten norms around tipping confuse many travelers.

cards.frontJa: Public ___ varies widely on trains between countries.
cards.backEn: Public etiquette varies widely on trains between countries.

cards.frontJa: ___ identity can feel fluid in multicultural cities.
cards.backEn: Cultural identity can feel fluid in multicultural cities.

cards.frontJa: Holidays ___ how a society marks time and loss.
cards.backEn: Holidays reveal how a society marks time and loss.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 偏見を減らすには対話が必要だ。
cards.backEn: Dialogue is needed to reduce prejudice.

cards.frontJa: ___ curiosity beats snap judgments about strangers.
cards.backEn: Honest curiosity beats snap judgments about strangers.

cards.frontJa: Some ___ prefer consensus before a public decision.
cards.backEn: Some cultures prefer consensus before a public decision.

cards.frontJa: ___ media literacy is now a civic skill.
cards.backEn: Basic media literacy is now a civic skill.

cards.frontJa: ___ listening helps more than giving quick advice.
cards.backEn: Patient listening helps more than giving quick advice.

cards.frontJa: It started ___ a misunderstanding about the deadline.
cards.backEn: It started with a misunderstanding about the deadline.

cards.frontJa: ___ morning, everything still felt normal.
cards.backEn: That morning, everything still felt normal.

cards.frontJa: ___ noon, rumors had spread across the office.
cards.backEn: By noon, rumors had spread across the office.

cards.frontJa: ___ evening, leadership called an emergency meeting.
cards.backEn: By evening, leadership called an emergency meeting.

cards.frontJa: ___ the end of the week, we had a new plan.
cards.backEn: By the end of the week, we had a new plan.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 振り返ると、あの一言が転機だった。
cards.backEn: In hindsight, that one sentence was the turning point.

cards.frontJa: ___ the crisis, the team actually grew closer.
cards.backEn: During the crisis, the team actually grew closer.

cards.frontJa: ___ a while, I wasn't sure we'd recover.
cards.backEn: For a while, I wasn't sure we'd recover.

cards.frontJa: ___ then, we've documented every decision.
cards.backEn: Since then, we've documented every decision.

cards.frontJa: ___ story matters because trust was on the line.
cards.backEn: The story matters because trust was on the line.

cards.frontJa: Translate: クライマックスは金曜の夜の会議だった。
cards.backEn: The climax was the meeting on Friday night.

cards.frontJa: ___ back, I'd call it a painful lesson.
cards.backEn: Looking back, I'd call it a painful lesson.

cards.frontJa: The ___: two people resigned anyway.
cards.backEn: The epilogue: two people resigned anyway.

cards.frontJa: ___ the narrator, I still wonder what we missed.
cards.backEn: As the narrator, I still wonder what we missed.

cards.frontJa: ___ tension built slowly, then broke all at once.
cards.backEn: The tension built slowly, then broke all at once.

cards.frontJa: ___ set the scene: it was raining in Osaka.
cards.backEn: Let me set the scene: it was raining in Osaka.

cards.frontJa: ___ character who mattered most was the quiet intern.
cards.backEn: The character who mattered most was the quiet intern.

cards.frontJa: ___ subplot involved a missing expense receipt.
cards.backEn: A subplot involved a missing expense receipt.

cards.frontJa: ___ turning point came when she spoke up.
cards.backEn: The turning point came when she spoke up.

cards.frontJa: ___ falling action, we renegotiated the contract.
cards.backEn: During the falling action, we renegotiated the contract.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 結末は bittersweet だった。
cards.backEn: The ending was bittersweet.

cards.frontJa: ___ on it, I should have asked more questions sooner.
cards.backEn: Reflecting on it, I should have asked more questions sooner.

cards.frontJa: ___ pace quickened once the investors arrived.
cards.backEn: The pace quickened once the investors arrived.

cards.frontJa: ___ flashback explains why he distrusts banks.
cards.backEn: A short flashback explains why he distrusts banks.

cards.frontJa: ___ foreshadowing was subtle, but it was there.
cards.backEn: The foreshadowing was subtle, but it was there.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 聞き手を飽きさせないには？
cards.backEn: How do we keep the listener engaged?

cards.frontJa: ___ hook I used was a single surprising statistic.
cards.backEn: The hook I used was a single surprising statistic.

cards.frontJa: ___ resolution wasn't perfect, but it was honest.
cards.backEn: The resolution wasn't perfect, but it was honest.

cards.frontJa: ___ coda: we still send that intern holiday cards.
cards.backEn: The coda: we still send that intern holiday cards.

cards.frontJa: ___ throughline was integrity, not profit.
cards.backEn: The throughline was integrity, not profit.

cards.frontJa: ___ do you mean by 'soon' — this week or this month?
cards.backEn: What do you mean by 'soon' — this week or this month?

cards.frontJa: Could you ___ that in simpler terms?
cards.backEn: Could you explain that in simpler terms?

cards.frontJa: Let me ___ what I meant — I wasn't blaming you.
cards.backEn: Let me clarify what I meant — I wasn't blaming you.

cards.frontJa: I want to make sure we're ___ about the same metric.
cards.backEn: I want to make sure we're talking about the same metric.

cards.frontJa: When you say 'stable,' do you ___ uptime or error rate?
cards.backEn: When you say 'stable,' do you mean uptime or error rate?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 言い換えると、コスト削減が目的か？
cards.backEn: In other words, is the goal to cut costs?

cards.frontJa: Sorry, I ___ you — could you repeat the last sentence?
cards.backEn: Sorry, I lost you — could you repeat the last sentence?

cards.frontJa: Can we ___ an example so I'm sure I follow?
cards.backEn: Can we walk through an example so I'm sure I follow?

cards.frontJa: I'm not ___ I understand the trade-offs yet.
cards.backEn: I'm not sure I understand the trade-offs yet.

cards.frontJa: Just to ___, you're proposing we skip QA?
cards.backEn: Just to confirm, you're proposing we skip QA?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 要点を三行でまとめてもらえる？
cards.backEn: Could you summarize the gist in three lines?

cards.frontJa: Let me ___ my point another way.
cards.backEn: Let me restate my point another way.

cards.frontJa: Which part feels ___ to you?
cards.backEn: Which part feels unclear to you?

cards.frontJa: I'll ___ my screen so we can annotate together.
cards.backEn: I'll share my screen so we can annotate together.

cards.frontJa: Before we move on, ___ we align on definitions?
cards.backEn: Before we move on, can we align on definitions?

items.promptJa: 「Although it was late, we stayed.」に近い文法は？
options: Although it was late, we stayed.
Although it was late, but we stayed.
It was although late, we stayed.
Although late was it, we stayed.

items.promptJa: 第二条件として自然なのは？
items.promptEn: Which is a natural second conditional?
options: If I have time, I would travel.
If I had time, I would travel.
If I had time, I will travel.
If I have time, I had traveled.

items.promptJa: 丁寧に反対を示す表現は？
options: You're completely wrong.
I'm not sure I agree with that conclusion.
Nobody agrees with you.
That is stupid idea.

items.promptJa: 原因と結果のつなぎとして自然なのは？
items.promptEn: Which links cause and result naturally?
options: It rained. Therefore, we canceled the picnic.
It rained. Because, we canceled the picnic.
It rained. Although, we canceled the picnic.
It rained. While, we canceled the picnic.

items.promptJa: 意見を述べる導入として自然なのは？
options: I believe that transparency builds trust.
I believing that transparency builds trust.
Believe I that transparency builds trust.
I believe transparency that builds trust.

items.promptJa: ディベートで両面を示す表現は？
options: Only one hand.
On the one hand … on the other hand …
On the one side … on the same side …
One hand other hand no.

items.promptJa: アップデートの書き出しとして自然なのは？
items.promptEn: Which is a natural update opener?
options: Quick update: we shipped the hotfix last night.
Quickly updating: ship hotfix night.
Update quick we ship hotfix.
Hotfix shipped quick update.

items.promptJa: 意味を確認する表現は？
options: What do you mean by that term?
What you mean is?
Mean what you?
You mean what term?

items.promptEn: Which is closest to “Let me clarify…”?
options: Let me confuse what I meant.
Let me clarify what I meant.
Let me clarifyment my meant.
Clarify let me not.

items.promptJa: while（対比）として自然なのは？
options: While I respect his work, I disagree with this decision.
While I respecting his work, I disagree.
While respect I his work, disagree.
While I respect his work, but I disagree.

items.promptJa: 叙述で場面を置く表現は？
items.promptEn: Which sets the scene in a story?
options: Let me set the scene: it was raining in Osaka.
Set me let scene raining Osaka.
Scene set let raining me.
Let scene me set Osaka raining.

items.promptJa: 文化的配慮を示す表現として自然なのは？
options: Everyone thinks the same everywhere.
Across cultures, direct feedback can sound rude.
Cultures no difference feedback.
Across culture feedback rude always.
